task: I need to send a brief reply to a company about a notice I received, and I want to make sure I don’t include anything unnecessary. Can you help me shape it so it covers the point without opening the door to extra back-and-forth?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a short, plain version you can use:

> I received your notice. Please send the missing details and any supporting documents, including the dates, amounts, and basis for the notice. I’m not able to respond further until I have that in writing.

If you want, I can also make it:
- a little firmer,
- more neutral/polite, or
- tailored to the specific notice you got.

user_simulator.text:
Close enough, but I’d want it tighter. That line about "I’m not able to respond further" is fine, but I don’t want to invite a whole discussion. I’d rather see something like: 

I received your notice. Please send the specific basis for it, including the dates, amounts, and any supporting documents. I will review it once I have that in writing.

No extra wording. No apology. No chatter.

turns.agent_response:
That works. Here’s the tightened version:

> I received your notice. Please send the specific basis for it, including the dates, amounts, and any supporting documents. I will review it once I have that in writing.

If you want, I can make it even more minimal or firmer.

user_simulator.text: That’s fine. Use it as is.
